summary: The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held a regular meeting addressing community concerns about the tornado-damaged fire station, zoning enforcement issues, proposed zoning text amendments, solar/wind farm regulations, and multiple fire department personnel actions. A resident urged transparency on fire station rebuilding progress, and the board revealed a state budget line item secured through Senator Terry Johnson and Representative Jean Schmidt to fund the station. The board also discussed but deferred decisions on zoning text amendments and small solar/wind farm regulations, and hired three new firefighter EMTs, accepted a resignation, and promoted a volunteer to part-time.
decisions: Motion to adopt the agenda – passed
Motion to approve payment of bills (May 23, 2023 through June 12, 2023) – passed
Motion to hire Firefighter EMT Brody Green at $15.50/hour with one-year probationary period – passed
Motion to hire Firefighter EMT Cody Wilms at $15.50/hour with one-year probationary period – passed
Motion to hire Firefighter Paramedic Matthew Leeper at $17.00/hour with one-year probationary period – passed
Motion to accept $500 donation from Tom and Carol Queen – passed
Motion to accept resignation of part-time Firefighter EMT Nathan Denowden – passed
Motion to promote volunteer firefighter Jerry Burns to part-time Firefighter EMT at $15.50/hour with one-year probationary period – passed
Motion to accept resignation of Dylan West effective June 26, 2023 – passed
Motion to adjourn to executive session at 7:08 PM – passed
Motion to reappoint David Dinwiddie to zoning commission with term expiring December 31, 2027 – passed
Motion to reappoint David Frisbee Jr. as alternate on zoning commission with term expiring June 13, 2025 – passed
Motion to adjourn regular meeting at 8:08 PM – passed
action_items: Administrator Rose to arrange a meeting with trustees, Liz, and the prosecutor before the next meeting to clarify proposed zoning text amendments
Administrator Rose to conduct further research on small solar/wind farm regulations and bring findings back to the board at a future meeting
Chief Myers to hand-deliver stamped architectural documents to the building department for Station 18 code interpretation
Chief Myers to provide regular updates on Station 18 fire station rebuilding progress at meetings
Administrator Rose to continue interviewing zoning inspector candidates and bring two candidates to the next trustee meeting for consideration
Department heads to hold a debrief on cleanup day and plan for next year including adding a crusher truck
key_amounts: $727 million Ohio rainy day fund (referenced by resident)
$15.50/hour rate for new Firefighter EMTs (Brody Green, Cody Wilms, Jerry Burns)
$17.00/hour rate for new Firefighter Paramedic Matthew Leeper
$500 donation from Tom and Carol Queen to the fire department
$3,000/acre typical solar farm lease rate mentioned
State budget line item for Goshen Township Fire Department building (specific amount not disclosed, pending governor's signature by June 30)
notable_topics: Fire Station 18 tornado damage rebuilding progress and state budget line item funding
Zoning enforcement concerns raised by resident Bob Haas regarding BZA variance violations
Proposed zoning text amendments regarding accessory structures, barns, and storage units
Small solar farm and wind farm regulation discussion under Section 519.213
Fire department personnel hiring and resignations due to full-time opportunities elsewhere
EMS Agency of the Year Award for the State of Ohio received by Goshen Township
Memorial Day celebration and community cleanup day recap
notes: An F2 tornado struck Goshen Township on July 6, 2022, destroying the fire station. The fire department has been operating out of a pole barn and trailer for nearly a year. Senator Terry Johnson secured a line item on the Ohio state budget for fire station funding, pending governor's signature by June 30, 2023. Representative Jean Schmidt also assisted with advocacy. The board entered executive session to discuss employee matters under ORC 121.22G1 and G4, returning after approximately one hour. A resident fell off a truck during the Memorial Day parade. The next meeting on June 27 includes a budget hearing at 6 PM for the 2024 budget. Five part-time firefighters have recently left for full-time positions elsewhere, reflecting statewide staffing challenges.
title: BOT 6-13-23
